title: Charred Molten Brazilian Meat Tagine
description: A Brazilian-inspired dish featuring charred and molten meat, served in a comal vessel. This low-carb-friendly recipe is perfect for a date-night dinner.
instructions: Mix together the garlic granules, cumin, and smoked paprika in a small bowl.
Season the flank steak with the spice mixture, making sure to coat it evenly.
Heat the comal over medium-high heat and sear the steak for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until a nice crust forms.
Transfer the comal to the preheated oven and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until the steak reaches your desired level of doneness.
While the steak is cooking, heat the coconut oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the sliced onions and cook until they are caramelized and golden brown, stirring occasionally.
Once the steak is done cooking, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes.
Slice the steak thinly against the grain and serve it with the caramelized onions and a sprinkle of cilantro.

ingredients.name: Flank steak
ingredients.notes: Choose a high-quality flank steak for the best results.

ingredients.name: Garlic granules
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount of garlic to your taste.

ingredients.name: Cumin
ingredients.notes: Ground cumin adds a warm and earthy flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika adds a nice smoky flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Coconut oil
ingredients.notes: Coconut oil adds a nice flavor and helps to prevent the steak from sticking to the comal.

ingredients.name: Onions
ingredients.notes: You can use a combination of yellow and red onions for a sweeter flavor.

ingredients.name: Cilantro
ingredients.notes: Chopped cilantro adds a fresh and herbaceous flavor to the dish.
